questions.q: What is the full name represented by the initials V.P. Singh?
questions.explanation: V.P. Singh stands for Vishwanath Pratap Singh.

questions.q: V.P. Singh served as Prime Minister of India during which period?
questions.explanation: V.P. Singh served as Prime Minister from 1989 to 1990.

questions.q: V.P. Singh headed which coalition as Prime Minister?
questions.explanation: He led the National Front coalition government as Prime Minister.

questions.q: V.P. Singh's government is most associated with implementing the recommendations of which commission?
questions.explanation: His government implemented the recommendations of the Mandal Commission on reservations.

questions.q: The Mandal Commission, implemented under V.P. Singh, dealt principally with which subject?
questions.explanation: The Mandal Commission examined backward classes and recommended reservations in central government jobs.

questions.q: V.P. Singh is given which descriptive title on the Katha Cards deck?
questions.explanation: He is titled 'Prime Minister of Mandal' for implementing the Mandal Commission recommendations.

questions.q: In which year did V.P. Singh become Prime Minister of India?
questions.explanation: V.P. Singh became Prime Minister in 1989.

questions.q: V.P. Singh's tenure as Prime Minister is best described as which of the following?
options: A tenure of about a year
questions.explanation: His tenure lasted only about a year, from 1989 to 1990.

questions.q: The recommendations implemented by V.P. Singh's government concerned reservations in which sphere?
questions.explanation: The Mandal recommendation implemented in 1990 concerned reservations in central government jobs.

questions.q: V.P. Singh became Prime Minister immediately after which leader's tenure?
questions.explanation: V.P. Singh became Prime Minister in 1989, succeeding Rajiv Gandhi.

questions.q: V.P. Singh is often described as which sequential Prime Minister of India?
questions.explanation: V.P. Singh is counted as the seventh Prime Minister of India.

questions.q: The Mandal Commission was originally set up in which year, well before V.P. Singh implemented its recommendations?
questions.explanation: The Mandal Commission was set up in 1979 and reported in 1980; V.P. Singh implemented it in 1990.

questions.q: The Mandal Commission's recommendation on reservations was implemented in which year?
questions.explanation: The recommendation was implemented in 1990 during V.P. Singh's tenure.

questions.q: The National Front, which V.P. Singh led, was an example of which kind of arrangement?
questions.explanation: The National Front was a coalition of parties that formed the government under V.P. Singh.

questions.q: V.P. Singh's premiership fell within which broader phase of Indian politics?
options: The era of coalition governments beginning in the late 1980s
questions.explanation: His 1989 government opened the era of coalition governments at the Union level.

questions.q: Before becoming Prime Minister, V.P. Singh had held senior office at the Union level in which capacity?
questions.explanation: He had earlier served as a senior Union cabinet minister before becoming Prime Minister.

questions.q: The reservations recommended by the Mandal Commission were aimed at which constitutional category of groups?
questions.explanation: The Commission examined socially and educationally backward classes.

questions.q: Which Prime Minister succeeded V.P. Singh in office in 1990?
questions.explanation: Chandra Shekhar succeeded V.P. Singh as Prime Minister in 1990.

questions.q: V.P. Singh's National Front government depended on which feature common to coalition politics of the period?
options: Support from parties outside the core coalition
A two-thirds majority of one party
Direct election of the Prime Minister
questions.explanation: Like other coalitions of the period, it depended on support from parties outside the core front.

questions.q: The implementation of the Mandal Commission recommendations is associated with reservations of what nature?
questions.explanation: It involved reservation of public employment, specifically central government jobs.

questions.q: V.P. Singh's tenure is counted among Prime Ministers who governed during which decade's turn?
questions.explanation: He governed at the turn of the 1990s, from 1989 to 1990.

questions.q: The coalition led by V.P. Singh came to power following which event?
questions.explanation: The National Front came to power following the 1989 general election.

questions.q: The Mandal Commission, whose recommendations V.P. Singh implemented, submitted its report in which year?
questions.explanation: The Mandal Commission, set up in 1979, submitted its report in 1980.

questions.q: V.P. Singh's premiership immediately preceded that of which short-tenured Prime Minister of the early 1990s?
questions.explanation: V.P. Singh's premiership immediately preceded that of Chandra Shekhar.

questions.q: The reservation policy implemented in 1990 was directed at which category in addition to existing reservations for Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes?
questions.explanation: The Mandal implementation extended reservations to Other Backward Classes in central government jobs.

questions.q: V.P. Singh's National Front government is an example of which post-1989 trend in Indian governance?
options: Formation of multi-party coalitions at the Union
questions.explanation: His government exemplifies the post-1989 trend of multi-party coalitions at the Union level.

questions.q: V.P. Singh assumed the office of Prime Minister through which constitutional route in 1989?
options: Nomination by the outgoing Prime Minister
questions.explanation: He was appointed Prime Minister by the President, the standard constitutional route.

questions.q: The Mandal Commission was constitutionally rooted in the State's mandate to address backwardness, a concern reflected in which part of the Constitution?
options: The Union List in the Seventh Schedule
The Directive Principles in Part IV
The citizenship provisions in Part II
questions.explanation: Concern for backward classes reflects the Directive Principles of State Policy in Part IV.

questions.q: V.P. Singh's term as Prime Minister came to a close in which year?
questions.explanation: His term as Prime Minister came to a close in 1990.

questions.q: Within the sequence of Indian Prime Ministers, V.P. Singh's National Front government followed a long phase of governments led by which party?
questions.explanation: His National Front government followed a long phase of Congress-led governments at the Union.
